pattern: Two pointers from the ends always discard the shorter wall, because moving the taller one can never increase a width-limited area.
hints: The water a pair of lines holds is the shorter line's height times the distance between them.
Start with the widest pair and ask which side to move inward without missing a better answer.
Place pointers at both ends. The area is min(height[l], height[r]) * (r - l); record it, then move the pointer at the shorter line inward - moving the taller one can only shrink or keep the area.

edge_inputs.description: tallest pair is not the widest pair
two tall walls far apart beat a near tall pair

stress.description: large random height array exposing any quadratic scan
stress.note: two-pointer must finish in linear time on 100k elements
